generation.image_prompt: Mystical 1900s lithography tarot card illustration. Clean bold black ink outlines. An anthropomorphic leopard cat standing defiantly on a rocky cliff edge, holding one large wooden wand horizontally as if to block a path. Six other wands are pointing UP toward it from below (unseen attackers). It looks fierce and determined. rosettes and white ear spots are clearly visible. Full bleed. No text. No borders. High-fidelity biological markers: narrow small-cat facial structure, distinct white stripes between the eyes, white muzzle, and large white spots behind black ears. 2:3 vertical. Full bleed. No borders.
generation.narrative: A leopard cat defends its hard-won territory. Even when outnumbered by development and pressure, it stands its ground to protect the core habitat.

meanings.upright: Defensiveness, standing ground, protection of assets/territory, and the courage to face overwhelming odds.
meanings.reversed: Giving up, being overwhelmed by pressure, or failing to maintain boundaries.

ecology.risk_theme: the struggle to hold shrinking habitat patches
ecology.mapping_note: Seven of Wands represents the 'Boundary Defense'—the vital need to protect the few remaining safe core zones from constant external pressure.

website.excerpt: Standing firm against the tide of development, the leopard cat defends its territory from a position of strength.
website.seo_description: Seven of Wands reimagined as leopard cat boundary defense and territorial protection.

meaning.zh: Defensiveness, standing ground, protection of assets/territory, and the courage to face overwhelming odds.
meaning.en: Defensiveness, standing ground, protection of assets/territory, and the courage to face overwhelming odds.
